_comment: Curated, hand-verified region requirements layered on top of tools/sim/data/quests.json + goals.json (docs/entrance-logic.md Workstream C). Deliberately NOT exhaustive - per the design brief, only goal-critical quests/goals get a real spatial anchor here; everything else is assumed reachable once the anchor's containing macro-region ('mainland') is reachable, matching progression-sim.md's own 'one connected mainland' travel model. Grow this file as more quests turn out to matter for goal reachability under a real shuffled seed.

mainland.note: Lumbridge, per the design brief. Region graph confirms this is the single largest surface region (id varies per build, see region-graph.json meta.mainlandRegionId).

karamja.note: Musa Point dock arrival tile, sailors.rs2:10 (`karamja_sailor_dialogue("Karamja", 1_46_49_12_7, ^sail_port_sarim_to_karamja)`). NPC-dialogue-triggered boat (~set_sail proc), NOT a ladders+stairs entrance - never touched by EntranceParser/RandomizeEntrances, always vanilla regardless of seed. Region-graph-verified as its own isolated region (not mainland) - see alwaysConnected below for why that's fine.

melzars_maze_approach.note: 'melzardoor' loc placement, m45_50.jm2 local(61,48). This is the OUTSIDE approach tile to the Melzar's Maze building (confirmed region-graph-reachable from mainland, id 37 in the reference build). Deliberately NOT anchored on the maze's interior (past the door) - melzars_maze.rs2's oploc1/oplocu handlers show the door itself requires `last_useitem = melzarkey`, an in-dungeon puzzle-key gate (colored-key rat drops, see melzars_maze.rs2:75-136), not an entrance-shuffle-affected transition. That puzzle is narrative-only here, matching quests.json's dragon entry's own 'items' field (not hard-gated) and progression-sim.md's documented item-requirement policy. What DOES matter for seed validation is whether the building's outside approach is still reachable on foot after entrance shuffle - that's what this anchor checks.

kbd_lair.note: King black dragon NPC placement, m42_153.jm2 (real mapZ 53, +100 underground/instanced convention) local(28,25). Region-graph-verified as its own isolated region.

entrana.note: High Priest's church tile, area_entrana. Reached ONLY by the Port Sarim monks' boat - monk_of_entrana.rs2:23 `~set_sail("Entrana", ..., 1_44_52_18_3, ^sail_port_sarim_to_entrana, 13)` - an NPC-dialogue transport exactly like the Karamja boat: never parsed by EntranceParser, never shuffled. Needed by grail (High Priest + crone) and hero (Entrana firebird feather).

crandor.note: Crandor surface next to the crandor_rock_opening loc (m43-44_50). Reached by the Dragon Slayer ship (Ned, dialogue + quest stage - crandor.rs2's own notes in the dragon quest entry apply: never in the ladders+stairs pool, never shuffled). Once the surface is reachable, the extracted world edges from crandor_rock_opening/crandor_climbing_rope (crandor.rs2 oploc p_teleports) connect the underground lair chain automatically.

kbd_lever_room.note: 'dragonkinginlever' loc placement, m47_160.jm2 local(59,13) - king_black_dragon.rs2's oploc1 handler for this loc does `~player_teleport_normal(0_42_153_29_10)`, landing directly in the kbd_lair region. This IS the real KBD entrance (a wilderness lever, not a ladders+stairs oploc), always vanilla/never shuffled.

lava_maze_mirror_landing.note: area_wilderness/scripts/lava_maze.rs2's 'wildymirrorladdertop2'/loc_1766 teleport pair (0_47_60_8_9 <-> 0_47_160_8_9), always vanilla/never shuffled (not in ladders+stairs). KNOWN GAP (documented, not silently dropped): this lands in a DIFFERENT region than kbd_lever_room within the same mapsquare m47_160 (region-graph-verified as two distinct regions) - i.e. this session traced the real always-vanilla KBD lever mechanism but did NOT finish tracing how a player physically walks from this mirror-ladder landing spot to the lever room inside the same instanced area (likely another internal connector - rope/further ladder/lava-safe-tile path - not yet found). Because of this, 'mainland' has NO verified alwaysConnected path to kbd_lever_room/kbd_lair yet, so the 'kbd' goal will show BLOCKED in ValidateSeed's output even though the underlying game mechanism (the lever) is real and always available. This is a graph-completeness gap in this file, not a seed-specific entrance-shuffle problem - see the session's final report for the explicit call-out. Follow-up: find the missing internal connector and add one more alwaysConnected hop (lava_maze_mirror_landing -> kbd_lever_room), or trace a shorter direct path.

alwaysConnected.note: The 30gp Port Sarim boat (sailors.rs2 ~set_sail) is NPC-dialogue-triggered, not a ladders+stairs oploc entrance - EntranceParser never sees it, so it is NEVER shuffled and always connects exactly as vanilla. Modeled as a free, unconditional, always-present edge between the two anchor regions (both directions) rather than a real graph edge, matching docs/progression-sim.md's own treatment of Karamja ('trivial fare... narrative only, never a hard gate').
Port Sarim monks' boat (monk_of_entrana.rs2 ~set_sail) - NPC-dialogue transport, never shuffled, same modeling convention as the Karamja boat.

Dragon Slayer ship via Ned (quest-stage dialogue transport, never shuffled). OPTIMISTIC per the documented convention: the ship exists once the quest reaches that stage; the validator's quest model tracks the stage requirements (maps, QP 32) separately.
lava_maze.rs2's mirror-ladder teleport pair, always vanilla/never shuffled (surface trigger 0_47_60_8_9 is ordinary wilderness overworld terrain, already part of mainland).

king_black_dragon.rs2's dragonkinginlever/dragonkingoutlever pair - a real, always-vanilla, never-shuffled entrance to the boss room. See kbd_lever_room's note for the still-missing link from lava_maze_mirror_landing to here.

generated._comment: Review lever over quest-regions.generated.json (see GeneratedQuestRegions.ts). ignoreGlobal: evidence keys exempted for every quest (shared-helper noise). ignore: per-quest key lists a human judged optional/flavor/alternative-route. Every entry should say WHY in _notes.

dragon.notes: Dragon Slayer's Crandor leg (Ned's ship dialogue, crandor.rs2 p_teleport literals 0_44_150_17_58 / 0_44_50_17_57) is ALSO NPC-dialogue-triggered and outside content/scripts/ladders+stairs/ - never shuffled, always available once the quest reaches that stage, so it needs no region anchor of its own. The one genuinely shuffle-sensitive physical requirement is being able to walk to the Melzar's Maze building at all.

kbd.notes: goals.json's kbd entry already encodes the 40/40/40/40 combat judgment call; this adds the spatial half - the lair region must actually be reachable. CURRENT STATE (honest, see kbd_lever_room's note): the real always-vanilla lever entrance IS traced and modeled, but the connector from mainland/wilderness surface into the lever's own chamber is not yet found, so this goal reports BLOCKED in ValidateSeed today. That is a known region-graph tracing gap for this one goal, not evidence the live seed is actually unbeatable - flagged explicitly rather than silently faked with an unverified edge.

barcrawl.notes: Two of the ten barcrawl bars (Dead Man's Chest in Brimhaven, Karamja Spirits) are on Karamja. Region-graph-checked via the alwaysConnected boat edge above, not a hard shuffle-sensitive gate (matching progression-sim.md's treatment) - included here so ValidateSeed's region layer agrees with the simulator's own documented judgment call instead of silently ignoring Karamja reachability.
